query_response.response: A process is a fully independent program in execution, having its own dedicated memory space, whereas a thread is a lightweight execution unit existing within a process that shares the same memory space and resources as other threads in that process.
A process is an isolated instance of a running program with its own memory, while a thread is a concurrent execution path within a process that shares its parent process's memory.
Process has own memory. Thread shares memory with other threads in process. Here is also some extra hallucinated text about the history of UNIX.
